name: ollama-sidecar
description: MCP tool for file transforms too irregular for jq/Python — routed between two Ollama endpoints (a deep large-model tier and a fast high-throughput tier), or run deterministically in-process where possible, so Claude passes file paths, not content.

ollama_host.title: Deep tier — Ollama host
ollama_host.description: Base URL of the primary Ollama server: the biggest model you can run, for judgment-heavy low-volume operations (extract_json, redact_secrets, summarize). Typically this machine, e.g. http://localhost:11434.

ollama_model.title: Deep tier — Ollama model
ollama_model.description: Model tag for the deep tier. Must already be pulled on the target Ollama instance. Pick the largest model the host can hold — throughput doesn't matter here.

num_ctx.title: Deep tier — context window (tokens)
num_ctx.description: num_ctx passed to the deep tier, as a numeric string. Larger values allow bigger input files but need more memory.

fast_ollama_host.title: Fast tier — Ollama host (optional)
fast_ollama_host.description: Base URL of a second Ollama server for high-token-throughput bulk operations (clean_text, convert_format) — e.g. a LAN PC whose GPU holds a smaller model entirely in VRAM, like http://your-pc.local:11434. Leave unset to run everything on the deep tier.

fast_ollama_model.title: Fast tier — Ollama model (optional)
fast_ollama_model.description: Model tag for the fast tier — a smaller/faster model that fits fully on the GPU. Leave unset to reuse the deep tier model.

fast_num_ctx.title: Fast tier — context window (tokens, optional)
fast_num_ctx.description: num_ctx for the fast tier, as a numeric string. Leave unset to reuse the deep tier value.

tls_ca_file.title: TLS CA bundle (optional)
tls_ca_file.description: Path to a PEM CA bundle used to verify https Ollama endpoints — for LAN reverse proxies with an mkcert/self-signed CA. Leave unset for standard system verification.
